_rationale: code_insight is a mode-dispatched tool. Go honours arguments.mode and emits a section-scoped LCF payload (mode=statistics → only STATISTICS section). C++ accepts the mode parameter, passes it to CodebaseIntelligenceEngine::analyze but the engine downstream always returns mode=overview output (verified iter-4 audit 2026-05-12: probed mode=statistics/structure/unified/git_analyze/git_hotspots on multi-lang corpus, C++ stdout's first LCF line was 'mode=overview' for all five, Go's was the requested mode). This is a real C++ bug filed under loop zRvo9CV23xZD; the descriptor locks the divergence so a future fix flips it back to green. We assert only the envelope (result.content[].type) and the LCF version prefix to confirm the tool ran; mode-specific body is ignored until the bug is fixed.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope shape (jsonrpc/result/content[].type='text') is required to match Go MCP spec. Both binaries emit a text content frame regardless of mode parsing — locking this catches catastrophic failures (no response, error frame, wrong content type) while tolerating the mode body divergence.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text is ignored because C++ emits the overview body (mode=overview\ntokens=90...) instead of the statistics body (mode=statistics\ntokens=70...). This is the locked C++-side bug. Once the engine honours the mode arg, narrow the ignore back to elapsed_ms/timestamps only. id/jsonrpc/elapsed_ms/server_pid are universal noise per existing MCP descriptor convention.
